research_question: Can moving the same 0.70 liquidity guard from ranking eligibility to the effective universe boundary materially reduce low-liquidity dependence and improve fixed-test robustness without changing the score itself?

guard_contract.guard_definition: Only rows that are already ranking_eligible_D0 and whose 20-day average amount cross-sectional percentile is >= 0.70 on the same signal_date may remain in the effective universe before formal ranking.
guard_contract.treatment_of_rows_below_guard: Remain in audit tables but must be excluded from the effective universe used by run-state ranking.

constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: TopK + cash retention + no backfill unchanged

engineering_experiment.note: Chunk-size observation is engineering-only and must not be interpreted as a strategy degree of freedom.

disallowed_changes: Do not change score features or score weights.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not change sample labels or return definitions.
notes: This round changes exactly one core dimension: move the same 0.70 liquidity guard from ranking eligibility to the effective universe boundary.
Rows filtered by the universe liquidity guard must remain visible in audit outputs and failure analysis.
